bugs.error_message: API 400 invalid_request_error: '`temperature` is deprecated for this model.' (claude-opus-4-8). Every draft_chapter/evaluate/review call exit 1.
bugs.root_cause: autonovel scripts were written for Sonnet and pass a 'temperature' field in the /v1/messages payload. Opus 4.8 rejects 'temperature'. After switching all models to claude-opus-4-8, every API call 400'd.
bugs.fix: Removed the 'temperature' line from every script payload (sed over *.py). Also made draft_chapter.call_writer raise resp.text on non-200 so the real API error is visible instead of a bare raise_for_status.

bugs.error_message: Polish pass damaged prose: ch_16 left with an orphaned em-dash line (' — *none but ourselves can free our minds.*'), dropped GHOST dialogue, and empty '**' markers; novel_score regressed 8.0 -> 7.0 in revision cycle 5.
bugs.root_cause: apply_cuts.py (mechanical OVER-EXPLAIN/REDUNDANT span deletion) and the per-cycle/per-review 'mechanical cleanup' step are NOT gated by per-chapter re-eval and are tuned for prose fiction. On this format-heavy book they delete spans, leaving dangling markdown and incoherent fragments. The full-novel judge score is too coarse to catch single-chapter breakage, so damage was committed under a passing cycle score.
bugs.fix: Killed the pass; reset prose chapters to the cycle-4 peak commit 7c9a2f8 (novel_score 8.0); hand-repaired ch_16's two cut passages; ran a precise odd-'**'/orphan scan (0 flags); rebuilt all formats. For future polish runs: exclude the appendices and skip apply_cuts, or gate every mechanical edit with a per-chapter re-eval+revert.

bugs.error_message: KDP paperback rejected: 'This text is outside the margins' (p13) + 'Insufficient gutter ... require at least 0.5"' (p118,140).
bugs.root_cause: convert_equations.py rewrote some formula lines to $$...$$ but several were INSIDE ``` code fences (ch_10 Score/I_v, ch_11 D_audit, ch_12 T_r, ch_01 R_ext/A_hl). pandoc rendered them as verbatim -> literal '$$' text that can't wrap -> ran past the 6in text block into the gutter/margin. (Also affected designed PDF + epub.)
bugs.fix: Unwrap-script removed the code fences around single-equation blocks -> real display math. eqbox redefined to scale wide equations with adjustbox(max width). Print preamble hardened: fvextra breaklines, ragged headings, small longtables, emergencystretch, hidelinks. Result: 0 overfull hboxes; epubcheck clean.

bugs.error_message: Album songs all start with quiet static and sound generic/samey
bugs.root_cause: Songs were generated via elevenlabs music.compose with a single prose prompt + force_instrumental=True. The prose prompt collapsed each track to the shared 'industrial-goth 808 ~70BPM' denominator (era washed out), and the model prepended a quiet vinyl-crackle/ambient lead-in (~-56dB head) identical on every track = 'static intro'.
bugs.fix: New tools/compose_songs.py uses composition_plan (MusicPrompt) instead: era instruments woven into positive_global_styles AND every SongSection's positive_local_styles; negative_global_styles bans 'long ambient intro/slow fade-in/static intro/vinyl-crackle intro/white-noise wash'; an Intro section forces 'cold start on the downbeat, beat from bar one'; output mp3_44100_192; master = silenceremove head + loudnorm I=-10 TP=-1 + alimiter. API constraints learned: (a) must NOT pass music_length_ms with composition_plan (length = sum of section durations); (b) force_instrumental only works with prompt, not composition_plan (use empty section lines + 'no vocals' negative styles).

bugs.error_message: gen_audiobook_script.py: API 400 '`temperature` is deprecated for this model.' then, after removing it, httpx.ReadTimeout on longer chapters ('The read operation timed out') killing the parse mid-run (died at ch_04, ch_08).
bugs.root_cause: (1) call_claude posted a 'temperature' field, rejected by claude-opus-4-8 (same class as the 2026-06-05 temperature bug, different script). (2) Non-streaming httpx.post with timeout=300 can't cover Opus generating a large speaker-attributed JSON for a 4-5k-word chapter (100-175 segments), so the single read times out.
bugs.fix: Removed 'temperature'. Rewrote call_claude to STREAM (stream:true, parse SSE content_block_delta text deltas) with a per-read Timeout(read=180) that resets on every chunk + 4 retries with backoff. Also made parse (skip existing ch{NN}_script.json unless FORCE_PARSE=1) and render (skip existing ch_NN.mp3 unless FORCE_RENDER=1) resume-safe so a mid-run failure or session teardown never re-bills completed work. Long background renders launched detached via nohup+disown so a session restart doesn't kill them.

bugs.error_message: Apple Podcasts Connect: 'An error has occurred. Try again later.' when submitting the podcast RSS feed; show got created (name pulled from feed) but Episodes tab stayed empty.
bugs.root_cause: The Cloud Run feed service (publishing/app/main.py) declared /feed.xml with FastAPI @app.get only. Apple's iTMS crawler issues HEAD requests to validate the feed; HEAD returned 405 Method Not Allowed, so Apple treated the feed as unreachable/invalid and did not ingest episodes. (Confirmed via Cloud Run request logs: userAgent 'iTMS' HEAD /feed.xml -> 405.)
bugs.fix: Changed the route to @app.api_route('/feed.xml', methods=['GET','HEAD']) (and '/' likewise) so HEAD returns 200. Redeployed. Verified HEAD and GET both 200, incl. with -A iTMS. Also pinned Cloud Run --min-instances 1 (in deploy.sh) so Apple's fetch never hits a cold start. Debug tip: `gcloud logging read 'resource.labels.service_name=... httpRequest.requestUrl:feed.xml' --format='value(httpRequest.requestMethod,httpRequest.status,httpRequest.userAgent)'` to see the crawler.

bugs.error_message: xcli.py raised an uncaught traceback instead of a clean message when X credentials (or tweepy) were absent: 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named tweepy' and bare XError on whoami.
bugs.root_cause: tweepy was imported inline and not a declared dependency, and the import wasn't wrapped, so ImportError escaped the XError handler in main(); creds were also only checked deep in a property.
bugs.fix: Added tweepy>=4.14 to pyproject deps; wrapped imports in _import_tweepy() that raises XError with a uv/pip hint; kept _require() raising XError for missing X_* keys. main() catches XError -> 'error: ...' with exit code, no traceback.

bugs.error_message: `xcli.py --dry-run follow @user` failed with 'Missing X credentials' and made a network call, unlike every other dry-run write which is creds/network-free.
bugs.root_cause: XClient.follow() called resolve_user() (which hits get_user, needing creds+network) BEFORE the dry_run guard.
bugs.fix: Moved the dry_run branch above resolve_user(); in dry-run just echo username.lstrip('@') instead of resolving it to an id.
